title: Adaptive bitrate (abr) adjustments for live over the top (ott) distribution
abstract: There is provided a mechanism for providing Adaptive Bit Rate (ABR) adjustments of media streams like video for live Over the Top (OTT) distribution without the need to monitor buffer fill levels, which is advantageous for live video distribution over IP networks. The invention is based on monitoring change Î in the received bitrate level BR(t) at a client device, and providing a predicted future bandwidth characteristics of the network based on the change. By monitoring the received bitrate and its rate of change over time, a prediction of (near future) available bandwidth in the network is advantageously provided. A drastic drop of the received bitrate may for instance indicate that the available bandwidth will become too low to sustain a currently received media stream, and the system can accordingly initiate a decision to request a lower video quality.
claims:
Claims 1 . A method for adaptive bitrate, ABR, adjustments in an IP network comprising: monitoring a received bitrate BR of a media stream with a preset bitrate BO defined by said media stream; determining a rate of change Î of said received bitrate BR; and adjusting said preset bitrate BO based on said rate of change Î. 2. A method according to claim 1 , wherein if said rate of change Î is negative said method further comprises: determining if the received bitrate BR is predicted to reach a predetermined minimum threshold level BWmin within a threshold time period Tx, and if so providing a new bitrate Bx, which is lower than the preset bitrate BO; and adjusting said preset bitrate BO to said new bitrate Bx. 3. A method according to claim 2, wherein said threshold time period Tx is selected to be shorter the closer the received bitrate level is to the minimum threshold level. 4. A method according to claim 1 or 2, wherein if said determined rate of change Î is positive or zero, said method further comprises: temporarily increasing a transfer bitrate of the video stream to a higher new bitrate Bx, determining if said new bitrate Bx is applicable; and if said new bitrate Bx is applicable adjusting said preset bitrate BO to said new bitrate Bx. 5. A method according to claim 4, wherein said step of temporarily increasing the transfer bitrate of the video stream comprises adding a forwards error correction, FEC, code to said media stream. 6. A method according to claim 4 or 5, wherein said step of temporarily increasing said transfer bitrate comprises 

 transferring said media stream with a momentarily higher bitrate by detaining said media stream and subsequently transmitting a short burst of data. 7. A method according to any of claims 5 - 6, wherein said determining if said new bitrate Bx is applicable is based on at least one of: said determined rate of change A for subsequent sample intervals is positive or zero, monitoring of packet drop ratio, and comparing of interpacket arrival time and interpacket transfer time. 8. A method according to any of claims 3 - 7, further comprising selecting said new bitrate Bx from a list of available bitrates. 9. A method according to any of claims 3 to 8, wherein if said determined new bitrate Bx is below a predetermined minimum value defined as a bitrate value which is too low to sustain live transfer of said media stream, said new bitrate Bx is selected based on manual choice or by a customer profile. 10. A method according to claim 9, wherein said manual choice or customer profile comprises at least one of selection whether to abandon live transfer, and selection of priority of quality level and/or prioritized media content. 1 1 . A method according to claim 9 or 10, where said customer profile is retrieved from an external source. 12. A method according to claim 1 , wherein if said determined rate of change Î is less than a predetermined value or zero, said step of adjusting said preset bitrate comprises maintaining said preset bitrate B. 13. A node in a communication network comprising means for performing a method according to any preceding claim. 14. A software module adapted to perform a method according to any of claims 1 to 12 when executed by a computer processor.
